title: The Empty Chair
scripture_text: He will wipe every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; mourning and crying and pain will be no more.
reflection: The chair is still there. So is the absence. Heaven does not erase; it transforms. One day, you will see them again, and the years between will feel like a long breath held.
body:
Set a place for them. Or do not. Both are fine. Some days the empty chair is unbearable. Some days it is a kind of company.

The promise is not that you will stop missing them. The promise is that one day, missing will be no more, because the one you miss will be standing right in front of you, and you will not be able to believe you ever lived a day without them.

That day is real. It is closer than it feels. Hold on for it.
prayer: Lord, I miss them. Today, I miss them so much. Hold the space open. I am coming. Amen.
